matrix.description: Polyethylene (PE) matrix. Non-magnetic thermoplastic polymer used as binder via hot-pressing. Exhibits negligible electromagnetic loss.

magnetic_absorber.description: Nd0.15-doped M-type barium ferrite (Nd0.15-BaM). Prepared via sol-gel self-propagating combustion and sintered at 850C. Nd3+ doping enhances magneto-crystalline anisotropy and lattice distortion, improving both magnetic and dielectric loss.

conductive_filler.description: Carbon nanotubes (CNTs), 8 wt% composited on Nd-BaM surface. Form conductive networks that dissipate electromagnetic waves via electronic polarization and resistive heating. Significantly enhance dielectric loss and widen absorption bandwidth.

lightweight_magnetic.description: Hollow Nd-BaM microspheres or BaM-coated cenospheres. Reduced density variant of Nd-BaM with weaker but still present magnetic loss. Trades electromagnetic performance for significant weight reduction.

_notes.data_source: Electromagnetic parameters are approximated from VNA waveguide measurements (8.2-18 GHz) reported in Wang et al., Materials 2024, 17, 3433. The PE:CNTs@Nd0.15-BaM=2:1 sample exhibited eps'~5-7, eps''~2-4, mu'~1.2-1.5, mu''~0.1-0.4 across the band. Values here represent frequency-independent effective averages.
_notes.mixing_rule: Linear volume-fraction mixing. Actual composites exhibit non-linear effects (percolation threshold, interfacial polarization) not captured by this approximation.
_notes.sign_convention: eps_r = eps_real - j*eps_imag (negative imaginary part). Same for permeability.
_notes.key_mechanism: The synergy between CNTs (dielectric loss via conductive network and electronic polarization) and Nd-BaM (magnetic loss via natural ferromagnetic resonance and eddy current loss) achieves magnetoelectric complementarity. Debye relaxation analysis (Cole-Cole semicircles) confirms that the 2:1 ratio maximizes polarization relaxation loss.

dielectric_filler.description: High-loss carbon-based dielectric filler (e.g., MWCNTs). Enhanced dielectric loss for broadband absorption.

magnetic_filler.description: Ferrite-type magnetic filler (e.g., carbonyl iron). Strong magnetic loss for impedance matching.

lightweight_magnetic_filler.description: Lightweight magnetic filler (e.g., hollow Fe3O4 microspheres). Lower density but weaker magnetic loss.

_notes.electromagnetic_parameters: All values are frequency-independent constant approximations. Real materials exhibit significant dispersion across 2-18 GHz.
_notes.mixing_rule: Linear volume-fraction mixing. Future versions may adopt Maxwell-Garnett or Bruggeman.
_notes.sign_convention: eps_r = eps_real - j*eps_imag (negative imaginary part). Same for permeability.
